name: Octopus Tidbits
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil fresh octopus for at least 10 minutes.
2. Remove the skin. The skin and suckers will slip off the tentacles when cooked.
3. Prepare cooked octopus for tidbits by grinding as you would hamburger meat. One grind is sufficient; no further tenderizing is necessary.
4. Mix ingredients together.
5. Form into balls no larger than 1 inch in diameter.
6. Roll each ball in breadcrumbs to coat.
7. Pan fry the coated balls in butter or margarine until golden brown.
8. Garnish with lemon, lime, tomatoes, or lettuce.
These delicious tidbits are wonderful for fancy buffets and freeze well. They make a hit at any party. Great for both the gourmet and home cook. It is difficult to say exactly how many servings this yields, as some people may eat them all quickly while others only consume a few if they know what they are eating. For a main meal, it should serve four.
